greeting: So the road has brought you this far. Not many make it to the grove with their wits still whole.
I have watched pilgrims come and go for longer than I care to remember. You have a look about you that the others lacked.
The temple does not welcome the unprepared. Sit with me a while and I will tell you what I know.
quest: Three trials guard the inner sanctum, and each is shaped by the pilgrim who faces it. The first tests your memory of mercy — every kindness you showed on the road, and every cruelty. The Grail forgets nothing.
There is a spring beneath the temple roots that has not flowed in a hundred years. The old texts say it will run again when a true seeker arrives. Perhaps you should find it and see what happens.
idle: The trees here are older than the temple. They remember when the Grail company first came to this place.
I hear the spring sometimes, deep underground. A faint trickle, like a heartbeat waiting to quicken.
The Guardian will test you. Do not mistake his silence for approval or his questions for mere ceremony.
